'''Luke Archibald Douglas''' (born 12 May 1986) is a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Lower Clarence Magpies]] in the [[Northern Rivers Regional Rugby League|NRRRL]].

Douglas played for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] from 2006 to 2016. He also played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] in 2017 and 2018, and on loan from the [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the 2019 [[RFL Championship|Championship]], after which he retired from the sport.

Through ancestral eligibility, he represented Scotland at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]], [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]] and the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]].

== Early career ==
Douglas was born in [[Yamba, New South Wales]], Australia to parents Archibald Christopher (Chris) and Patricia (Trish) Douglas. He is of Scottish descent through his paternal grandfather, Archibald, who moved from [[Glasgow]] to [[Maclean, New South Wales]].<ref name=archibald>{{cite news|last=Robinson|first=Georgina|date=16 November 2013|title=Kane Douglas embraces life after mum's death|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/rugby-union/kane-douglas-embraces-life-after-mums-death-20131116-2xnbh.html|newspaper=The Sydney Morning Herald|accessdate=7 March 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Ferrie|first=Kevin|date=24 October 2013|title=Mum's the word as Douglas seeks happier ending|url=http://www.heraldscotland.com/sport/13128605.Mum_s_the_word_as_Douglas_seeks_happier_ending|newspaper=The Herald (Scotland)|accessdate=27 October 2013}}</ref> Douglas' brother is [[Australia national rugby union team|Australian rugby union]] representative [[Kane Douglas]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Tucker|first=Jim|date=13 September 2012|title=Titans NRL star Luke Douglas cheering on brother Wallaby Kane
|url=http://www.adelaidenow.com.au/sport/titans-nrl-star-luke-douglas-cheering-on-brother-wallaby-kane/news-story/98f1e8330a0b4e0a3afc7324d9ae78c3|newspaper=Adelaide Now}}</ref>

Douglas played junior rugby league for the Yamba Eagles and Lower Clarence Magpies.

==Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks==
Douglas made his first-grade debut for Cronulla in its season-opener against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in round 2, 2006.<!-- Cronulla had a bye in Round 1 --><ref>{{cite web|last=White|first=Tony|date=18 March 2006|title=A Shark is born|url=https://www.dailyexaminer.com.au/news/apn-a-shark-is/137335|publisher=Daily Examiner|accessdate=7 March 2018}}</ref>

In 2007, Douglas was selected for Country Origin but withdrew by claiming “injury” at the request of his club coach, Ricky Stuart.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/stuarts-hoax-still-troubles-douglas/story-e6frep5x-1226240288654|title=Titans recruit Luke Douglas reveals representative jumper angst after fake injury|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://archive.today/20140310104008/http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/stuarts-hoax-still-troubles-douglas/story-e6frep5x-1226240288654|archive-date=March 10, 2014|date=January 12, 2012|first=Steve|last=Ricketts|work=[[The Courier-Mail]]}}</ref> Controversially, Douglas then played 24 hours later for [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]].

In 2010, he was picked as 18th man for the New South Wales State of Origin team for the third match of the series, but he didn't play. He later played the only domestic representative match of his career for Country Origin in 2012.

Between his first grade debut in round 2, 2006 and round 23, 2014, inclusive, he never missed a first-grade match through injury or suspension. His run of 215 games straight is the NRL record for most consecutive appearances,<ref>{{cite web|title=State of No-rigin: the best never picked|url=http://www.nrl.com/state-of-no-rigin-the-best-never-picked/tabid/10874/newsid/78479/default.aspx|work=NRL.com|date=16 May 2014}}</ref> and the most from debut.<ref name="ritchie" /> He surpassed the previous record of 194, held by [[Jason Taylor (rugby league)|Jason Taylor]], on 10 March 2014 against his former club, [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]]. His coach, [[John Cartwright (rugby league)|John Cartwright]], said his record was "remarkable, especially playing in the front row - he plays pretty big minutes as well."<ref name="ritchie">{{cite news|author=Dean Ritchie|newspaper=Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|title=Gold Coast Titans prop Luke Douglas eyes consecutive games record|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/gold-coast-titans-prop-luke-douglas-eyes-consecutive-games-record/story-e6frexnr-1226305641073|accessdate=25 April 2012|date=21 March 2012}}</ref>

On 20 April 2011, it was announced that Douglas had signed a three-year contract with the [[Gold Coast Titans]].<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20121112183026/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2011/04/20/3197186.htm?site=sport&section=rugbyleague ABC Sport - Titans snap up Douglas]</ref>

Douglas’s unbroken streak of NRL matches since came to an end in August 2014 when he was one of several past and present Sharks players to accept a year-long ban, backdated to 21 November 2013, as part of [[Australian Sports Anti-Doping Authority|ASADA]]'s investigation into the club's [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga|controversial supplements program]].<ref>[http://www.goldcoastbulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/the-asada-ban-has-ended-gold-coast-titan-luke-douglas-record-run-of-215-consecutive-nrl-games/story-fnj9yd9w-1227033147890 The ASADA ban has ended Gold Coast Titan Luke Douglas’ record run of 215 consecutive NRL games], ''Gold Coast Bulletin'', 22 August 2014</ref>

==Gold Coast Titans==
After four turbulent years at the Titans, during which the club failed to qualify for the finals and was rocked by numerous scandals, Douglas departed the Gold Coast and signed a three-year deal with [[Super League]] club [[St Helens R.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37497014|title=St Helens sign Gold Coast Titans forward|work=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-02-24/nrl-takes-over-control-of-gold-coast-titans-amid-drugs-scandal/6247574/|title=Gold Coast Titans taken over by NRL, placed in voluntary administration after drugs scandal|newspaper=ABC News|date=24 February 2015}}</ref>

==St Helens==
On 25 January 2019, it was confirmed that St Helens had an agreed a one-month loan deal with [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] for Douglas.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-confirm-luke-douglas-loan-deal/|title=Leigh confirm Luke Douglas loan deal|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 January 2019}}</ref>
